summary: Iteration 8 — Battle v2 (hero-based turns + dynamic AP + per-consult calls + Rapid Response gating + rebound + related-systems) tested end-to-end on mobile 390x844 against https://med-adventure-2.preview.emergentagent.com/battle?enemyId=air_sprite using a freshly-seeded nursingStudent/guardian player (id c199601a-...). Backend regression: 31/31 pytest pass (test_player_api 16/16, test_iteration3_fields 6/6, test_iteration6_clinical_fields 10/10, 5.40s). NO backend regressions. PASSES: hero-pill row renders with testIDs hero-pill-novice_guardian and hero-pill-village_caretaker (player has 2 heroes_owned, not 3 — spec'd 3 only true if player has explicit active_team of size 3); first pill auto-selected with gold border colored by element ✓; initial AP=4 dots rendered ✓; Actions tab initially shows ONLY Novice Guardian's 3 skills (breath_of_dawn, lantern_of_clues, rapid_response) — NOT the whole team's pile ✓; after Breath of Dawn the NG pill subtitle flips to 'ACTED' and goes dim ✓; Actions tab then correctly shows 'Novice Guardian has already acted. Pick another hero or end the turn.' ✓; tapping the ACTED NG pill is a no-op (!acted gate works) ✓; Call Pharmacy does NOT consume a hero action — Village Caretaker pill stays Ready after the call ✓; Call Pharmacy consumed 2 AP (3→1) ✓. FAILURES (THREE CRITICAL UI BUGS — REPORT TO MAIN AGENT): (B1) Hero pill tap does NOT switch selection. Clicking [data-testid="hero-pill-village_caretaker"] leaves selectedHeroId on novice_guardian — the Actions tab keeps showing NG's skills (breath_of_dawn / lantern_of_clues / rapid_response) instead of VC's skills, and the NG pill keeps the gold selected border. Reproduced cleanly TWICE (before any action and after NG acted). This is the central feature of v2 and is broken end-to-end. Suspect: Pressable.onPress closure or RN-Web tap interception on the inner ScrollView heroRow. (B2) After ANY call, ALL other calls disappear. Root cause confirmed by code review: battle.tsx:118 — `availableCalls = CALL_OPTIONS.filter(opt => { if (state.callUsed) return false; ... })` uses the LEGACY single-flag `state.callUsed` instead of per-consult `state.callsUsed[callKey]`. After Call Pharmacy, `state.callUsed=true` (set in battle.ts:537) and Call Respiratory disappears from the Call tab (CALL_OPTIONS_AFTER_PHARMA=[]). The agent_to_agent_context says 'per-consult tracking (pharmacy/respiratory/rapidResponse/infectionControl)' and battle.ts already maintains callsUsed correctly — only the UI filter is wrong. (B3) Rapid Response is NOT shown when gated. Spec: 'While stability is above 30, the Rapid Response call button shows the badge "Reserved for Stability ≤ 30" and is disabled.' Current code battle.tsx:120 — `if (opt.id === "call_rapid") return state.stability <= 30` — HIDES the option entirely. The disabled+badge code paths exist (battle.tsx:440 rapidGated, :458 'Reserved for Stability ≤ 30') but never render because the option is filtered out beforehand. Verified: with stability=50, no [data-testid="call-opt-call_rapid"] in DOM and no 'Reserved for Stability' text anywhere. The b.ts engine guard (battle.ts:523) already prevents activation, so the UI filter is redundant AND violates the spec. ALSO DEFERRED (could not test due to B1+B2): items-tab discount label ('Prepared · ' prefix and reduced AP cost) was not visually verified because after Pharmacy no further calls could be issued and B1 blocks switching to VC; engine-side discount logic in useItem (battle.ts:403) is correct on code review. RELATED-SYSTEMS modifier and REBOUND mechanic verified statically — clinical.ts:602-611 getSystemMatchModifier returns 0.25 for unrelated systems (Forge/Energy vs Air enemy whose RELATED_SYSTEMS['Air']=['River','Protection']) and 1.0 for River, 0.5 for Fire (resistance), 1.5 for Air (weakness) — matches spec exactly. Rebound at battle.ts:609-617 fires when reboundArmed && !reassessUsed, applies +10 enemy damage and corruption +10, and logs the exact spec'd message '⚠ Rebound Bronchospasm: the disease surges back. Stability and Corruption worsen.' (battle.ts:611). Could not live-test the full rebound loop because B1 prevents acting with a second hero in the same turn (would need two-hero coordination to drop corruption < 40 without Reassess in one turn).

ui_bugs.issue: Tapping a non-selected ready hero pill does NOT update selectedHeroId — Actions tab keeps showing the original hero's skills. Verified twice on Village Caretaker pill. Blocks the entire one-action-per-hero feature. Reference: /app/frontend/app/battle.tsx:286-300 + /app/frontend/src/game/battle.ts:167.

After any single call, all other call options disappear because filter uses legacy `state.callUsed` flag instead of per-consult `state.callsUsed[callKey]`. Violates per-consult spec. Reference: /app/frontend/app/battle.tsx:117-123 (line 118 `if (state.callUsed) return false;`).

Rapid Response button is HIDDEN entirely when stability > 30 (battle.tsx:120 `return state.stability <= 30`). Spec requires the button to render in a disabled state with the badge 'Reserved for Stability ≤ 30'. The disabled-state code (battle.tsx:440 + 458) is correct but unreachable because of the upstream filter.

integration_issues.issue: selectHero() update may not be propagating to UI re-render — either Pressable.onPress is not firing on hero pills inside the horizontal ScrollView (possible RN-Web touch interception) or selectedHeroId state update is being lost. Manual repro: open /battle?enemyId=air_sprite, tap hero-pill-village_caretaker — Actions tab content unchanged, NG keeps gold border.

issues: Hero pill subtitle shows hero.element (e.g. RIVER for Novice Guardian) but NG's primary skill 'Breath of Dawn' is Air-typed and the badge says 'Air Stabilize'. Cosmetic inconsistency between hero element and skill systems — confusing for learners but not v2-spec related.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: 'Animated: useNativeDriver is not supported because the native animated module is missing. Falling back to JS-based animation.' Pre-existing web-preview limitation, not blocking.

Console warning: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' Multiple files use deprecated shadowColor/shadowOffset/shadowOpacity/shadowRadius instead of the new boxShadow API. Non-blocking but should migrate.
Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Non-blocking.

action_items: CRITICAL (B1): Fix hero-pill tap not switching selection. Debug Pressable onPress handler in battle.tsx:286-300 — verify selectHero() is being invoked and selectedHeroId in BattleState actually updates. Add a console.log inside onPress for diagnostics. Possibly the inner ScrollView's keyboardShouldPersistTaps='handled' is interfering, or the inline arrow function `() => !acted && setState(prev => selectHero(prev, h.id))` has a stale `acted` closure. Consider extracting to a named handler with explicit fresh-state check.

CRITICAL (B2): In /app/frontend/app/battle.tsx:117-123 replace the global `if (state.callUsed) return false;` with per-consult key lookup. Suggested fix: change the filter to determine callKey first (same mapping as battle.ts:511) and `if (callKey && state.callsUsed[callKey]) return false;` instead of returning false for the whole list. Better yet, KEEP all calls in the list and let the existing `alreadyUsed` flag + badge handle visual state.

HIGH (B3): In /app/frontend/app/battle.tsx:120 remove the `return state.stability <= 30` line for call_rapid. The button should ALWAYS render so the existing `rapidGated` disable + 'Reserved for Stability ≤ 30' badge (battle.tsx:440,458) can show. Engine-side guard at battle.ts:523 already prevents activation.

After B1+B2+B3 are fixed, retest: (a) tap VC pill → Actions tab shows VC's skills; (b) Use Call Pharmacy then verify Respiratory + Rapid Response still listed; (c) verify Rapid Response shows the 'Reserved for Stability ≤ 30' badge & is disabled at stability=50; (d) drop stability below 30 (e.g. via repeated end-turns), confirm Rapid Response becomes enabled and on activation grants +10 stability, shieldNext=100, and next-turn AP ≥ 4; (e) reduce corruption below 40 without using Reassess, end turn, verify '⚠ Rebound Bronchospasm' log + corruption +10 stability -10.
critical_code_review_comments: battle.tsx:117-123 — `availableCalls` filter uses the obsolete `state.callUsed` flag; battle.ts:537 still sets that flag for backward compat, but the v2 architecture's per-consult tracking is in `state.callsUsed`. The filter should defer to `callsUsed[callKey]` only, or simply not filter on usage at all and rely on the per-button `alreadyUsed` disable + badge.

battle.tsx:120 — `if (opt.id === "call_rapid") return state.stability <= 30` directly contradicts the spec ('Rapid Response call button shows the badge "Reserved for Stability ≤ 30" and is disabled'). Remove this branch; the engine guard at battle.ts:523-525 is the only enforcement needed.

battle.tsx:286-300 — Hero pill Pressable's onPress is the suspect for B1. The inline `!acted && setState(prev => selectHero(prev, h.id))` is logically correct, but live testing shows tap is not flipping selectedHeroId. Worth wrapping in useCallback or adding `hitSlop` (touch target is 40px tall — under the 44px minimum) and explicit `accessibilityRole="button"`.
battle.tsx:288 — Touch target height is 40px (styles.heroPill height). Per Apple HIG / Material 44dp minimum — should be ≥44.

Cosmetic: Novice Guardian's hero.element is 'River' but its starter skill Breath of Dawn is systemType 'Air'. The pill subtitle (h.element.toUpperCase) shows 'RIVER' which conflicts with the Air-typed badge displayed on the skill card. Confusing for learners. Reference: /app/frontend/src/game/content.ts (hero definition) vs SKILL_CLINICAL.breath_of_dawn (clinical.ts:124-128).

battle.ts:537 — keeps the legacy `callUsed: true` flag for backwards compat. With v2's `callsUsed` map active, the legacy flag is dead weight and is the root cause of B2. Consider removing it after fixing the UI filter.

success_rate.frontend: ~55% — 8/14 v2 specs verified pass (hero pills render, first auto-selected with gold border, AP=4 dots, Actions tab scoped to selected hero, ACTED state + opacity, ACTED tap is no-op, calls don't consume hero actions, Call Pharmacy costs 2 AP). 3 CRITICAL fail (hero pill switching, post-call list collapse, Rapid Response gating UI). 3 deferred due to blockers (items discount label render, rebound live test, multi-hero turn flow).

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_v2battle (id c199601a-6cf6-4e55-bf75-32dc2eb638dd, aptitude=guardian, profile=nursingStudent) via POST /api/player. Persisted to MongoDB. Recommend cleanup along with prior TEST_chain/TEST_win/etc.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Three concrete BUGs (B1 hero-pill switch, B2 per-consult filter, B3 Rapid Response gating) need a fix from main agent before retesting. Reproduction: seed a guardian/nursingStudent player via POST /api/player, set localStorage['clinica.playerId.v1']=<id>, navigate to /battle?enemyId=air_sprite on 390x844. B1: tap hero-pill-village_caretaker — Actions tab should switch to VC's skills (currently does not). B2: tap call-opt-call_pharmacy then go back to Call tab — call-opt-call_respiratory should still be listed (currently empty). B3: stability defaults to 50 — call-opt-call_rapid should be visible but disabled with 'Reserved for Stability ≤ 30' badge (currently missing entirely).
rca_of_issue: B1 RCA: battle.tsx:288 Pressable onPress=`() => !acted && setState(prev => selectHero(prev, h.id))`. The `acted` variable is closed over from line 282 (`const acted = !!state.heroActionsUsed[h.id]`) at render time. For Village Caretaker (h.id=village_caretaker), heroActionsUsed[village_caretaker]=false initially → acted=false → `!acted && setState(...)` should fire setState with selectHero. selectHero (battle.ts:167) checks team membership (VC is in team) and returns `{...s, selectedHeroId: heroId}`. Should work. The fact that it doesn't suggests either (a) the Pressable's onPress is not firing on web for items inside the horizontal ScrollView (RN-Web touch event issue), or (b) the closure is stale, or (c) the ScrollView container is intercepting taps. Recommend adding a temporary console.log inside the onPress and a debug `selectedHeroId: {state.selectedHeroId}` Text element on screen to bisect. B2 RCA: battle.tsx:118 — `if (state.callUsed) return false;` uses the v1 single-call flag. Engine still sets `callUsed: true` in battle.ts:537 for backwards compat with old screens. Fix: change to per-consult check or remove the filter entirely and rely on the per-button alreadyUsed + disabled state. B3 RCA: battle.tsx:120 — `if (opt.id === "call_rapid") return state.stability <= 30;` was a stale v1 visibility rule. v2 spec demands the button always visible with conditional disable + badge. The UI already supports this state (battle.tsx:440 sets rapidGated and :458 renders 'Reserved for Stability ≤ 30'). Fix: change `return state.stability <= 30` to `return true` (or remove the branch).
